messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following language learning app privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"LinguaApp collects your name, email, learning progress, and voice recordings of your spoken practice to provide language coaching. Voice recordings are analysed to assess pronunciation and are deleted after 30 days. We process this under contract with you. Progress data is retained for the duration of your subscription plus 1 year. You have the right to access, correct, port, restrict, erase, and object to your data. Contact privacy@linguaapp.com. DPO: dpo@linguaapp.com. We use Google Cloud (EU region) for processing and storage under a signed DPA. We notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) stated for language coaching service delivery.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Voice recordings can constitute biometric data under Art. 9 if used for unique identification purposes. The notice states recordings are used for pronunciation assessment, not identification, which reduces the Art. 9 risk — but this distinction should be explicitly stated to confirm no biometric template is created.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Pronunciation assessment and learning progress tracking are clear and specific purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] 30-day deletion of voice recordings after analysis is a strong minimisation measure.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Voice recordings: 30 days. Progress data: subscription duration plus 1 year. Both clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21) all listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Google Cloud EU region named as processor with signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EU region for Google Cloud confirmed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO at dpo@linguaapp.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour supervisory authority notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (biometric risk of voice recordings not explicitly excluded)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Add a statement explicitly confirming that voice recordings are not used to create biometric identification templates
